record.field: NetBox is a self-hosted, open-source IPAM and DCIM platform that models your network and data center as a single, authoritative source of truth.

NetBox is a self-hosted, open-source IPAM and DCIM platform that models your network and data center as a single, authoritative source of truth.

NetBox lets infrastructure teams document everything about their environment: IP addresses and prefixes, VLANs and VRFs, racks and devices, cabling and circuits, tenants, contacts and services. It replaces spreadsheets and tribal knowledge with a structured, queryable database and a full REST and GraphQL API, so both people and automation pipelines can rely on the same accurate model of the network.

Key features
  • IP address management (IPAM) with hierarchical prefixes, VLANs, VRFs and RIR/aggregate tracking
  • Data center infrastructure management (DCIM): sites, racks, devices, modules, power and cabling
  • Circuits, tenants, contacts and virtualization inventory for a complete asset picture
  • REST and GraphQL APIs, webhooks and custom scripts to drive network automation
  • Custom fields, tags, config contexts and change logging on every object
  • Granular permissions, token-based auth and a full audit trail of who changed what
Best for

Network and infrastructure teams that need an authoritative source of truth for IPs, racks and devices, and want to feed that model into automation. Restrict ALLOWED_HOSTS to your real domain and back it up often, as it quickly becomes critical documentation.

services: postgres: image: postgres:17-alpine restart: unless-stopped healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -q -d $${POSTGRES_DB} -U $${POSTGRES_USER} start_period: 20s interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 environment: POSTGRES_DB: {{ db_name }} POSTGRES_USER: {{ db_user }} POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ db_pass }} volumes: - ./database:/var/lib/postgresql/data expose: - 5432 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' networks: - internal redis: image: valkey/valkey:8.1-alpine restart: unless-stopped command: - sh - -c - valkey-server --appendonly yes --requirepass "$$REDIS_PASSWORD" healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - valkey-cli --pass "$${REDIS_PASSWORD}" ping | grep -q PONG start_period: 10s interval: 10s timeout: 3s retries: 5 environment: REDIS_PASSWORD: {{ redis_pass }} volumes: - ./redis:/data expose: - 6379 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_redis' networks: - internal netbox: image: netboxcommunity/netbox:latest restart: unless-stopped user: unit:root environment: DB_HOST: postgres DB_NAME: {{ db_name }} DB_USER: {{ db_user }} DB_PASSWORD: {{ db_pass }} REDIS_HOST: redis REDIS_PASSWORD: {{ redis_pass }} REDIS_DATABASE: '0' REDIS_SSL: 'false' REDIS_CACHE_HOST: redis REDIS_CACHE_PASSWORD: {{ redis_pass }} REDIS_CACHE_DATABASE: '1' REDIS_CACHE_SSL: 'false' SECRET_KEY: {{ secret_key }} ALLOWED_HOSTS: '{{ allowed_hosts }}' SUPERUSER_NAME: {{ superuser_name }} SUPERUSER_EMAIL: {{ superuser_email }} SUPERUSER_PASSWORD: {{ superuser_password }} SKIP_SUPERUSER: 'false' HOUSEKEEPING_INTERVAL: '86400' healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - curl -f http://localhost:8080/login/ || exit 1 start_period: 120s interval: 30s timeout: 5s retries: 5 volumes: - ./media:/opt/netbox/netbox/media - ./reports:/opt/netbox/netbox/reports - ./scripts:/opt/netbox/netbox/scripts expose: - 8080 depends_on: postgres: condition: service_healthy redis: condition: service_healthy container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8080' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network netbox-worker: image: netboxcommunity/netbox:latest restart: unless-stopped user: unit:root command: - /opt/netbox/venv/bin/python - /opt/netbox/netbox/manage.py - rqworker environment: DB_HOST: postgres DB_NAME: {{ db_name }} DB_USER: {{ db_user }} DB_PASSWORD: {{ db_pass }} REDIS_HOST: redis REDIS_PASSWORD: {{ redis_pass }} REDIS_DATABASE: '0' REDIS_SSL: 'false' REDIS_CACHE_HOST: redis REDIS_CACHE_PASSWORD: {{ redis_pass }} REDIS_CACHE_DATABASE: '1' REDIS_CACHE_SSL: 'false' SECRET_KEY: {{ secret_key }} ALLOWED_HOSTS: '{{ allowed_hosts }}' healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - ps -aux | grep -v grep | grep -q rqworker || exit 1 start_period: 20s interval: 30s timeout: 3s retries: 5 volumes: - ./media:/opt/netbox/netbox/media - ./reports:/opt/netbox/netbox/reports - ./scripts:/opt/netbox/netbox/scripts depends_on: netbox: condition: service_healthy container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_worker' networks: - internal netbox-housekeeping: image: netboxcommunity/netbox:latest restart: unless-stopped user: unit:root command: - /opt/netbox/housekeeping.sh environment: DB_HOST: postgres DB_NAME: {{ db_name }} DB_USER: {{ db_user }} DB_PASSWORD: {{ db_pass }} REDIS_HOST: redis REDIS_PASSWORD: {{ redis_pass }} REDIS_DATABASE: '0' REDIS_SSL: 'false' REDIS_CACHE_HOST: redis REDIS_CACHE_PASSWORD: {{ redis_pass }} REDIS_CACHE_DATABASE: '1' REDIS_CACHE_SSL: 'false' SECRET_KEY: {{ secret_key }} ALLOWED_HOSTS: '{{ allowed_hosts }}' HOUSEKEEPING_INTERVAL: '86400' healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - ps -aux | grep -v grep | grep -q housekeeping || exit 1 start_period: 20s interval: 30s timeout: 3s retries: 5 volumes: - ./media:/opt/netbox/netbox/media - ./reports:/opt/netbox/netbox/reports - ./scripts:/opt/netbox/netbox/scripts depends_on: netbox: condition: service_healthy container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_housekeeping' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump lógico consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR. # Con POSTGRES_USER/DB vacíos caemos a 'netbox' (default del template), evitando # que pg_dump intente el rol "root" del SO y falle con «role "root" does not exist». docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "${POSTGRES_USER:-netbox}" -d "${POSTGRES_DB:-${POSTGRES_USER:-netbox}}" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura (mismo default a 'netbox') docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "${POSTGRES_USER:-netbox}"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "${POSTGRES_USER:-netbox}" -d "${POSTGRES_DB:-${POSTGRES_USER:-netbox}}" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \${POSTGRES_USER:-netbox} -d \${POSTGRES_DB:-netbox} -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
PostgreSQL database that stores the NetBox data.
Password protecting the Redis/Valkey queue and cache.
Django SECRET_KEY used for cryptographic signing.
Username of the initial administrator account.
Email address of the initial administrator account.
Password for the initial administrator account.
Space-separated hostnames NetBox will answer to. Set your real domain in production instead of '*'.
